sources.notes: Count multisets S obtainable by partitioning a multiset and taking a mode from each part.
Official tutorial derives the condition max_{x not in S} cnt_x <= sum_{c in S} cnt_c and then uses vanilla knapsack DP.

annotation.summary: Count all output multisets that can be generated by splitting the input multiset and choosing one mode from each part.
annotation.constraints: Sum of n is 5000, enabling O(n^2) DP over total selected frequency.
annotation.core_idea: A color not selected in S must be hidden inside groups whose selected modes can cover its occurrences. A selected color c can provide at most cnt_c hiding capacity, so S is feasible exactly when the maximum frequency among unselected colors is at most the total frequency of selected colors. Thus count weighted choices of selected colors by total selected frequency with knapsack; each chosen color contributes cnt_c possible multiplicities in S.
tricks: mode feasibility becomes a frequency capacity condition

solution_variants.name: frequency-knapsack
solution_variants.summary: Run knapsack over distinct values with item weight cnt_c and multiplicity factor cnt_c, then sum states with selected total at least max_cnt.

tags.evidence: The answer is counted by a subset-style DP over total selected frequency.
The DP counts different output multisets and weights each selected value by the number of possible occurrences.
The official discussion allows processing counts in sorted/frequency order, though the vanilla DP only needs max_cnt.
